commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer action is needed for real proof, conflict resolution, and the permission/security fixes before this can be considered merge-ready.; Needs attention: The diff has a concrete filesystem-permission regression risk in the new atomic replacement helpers.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve target modes when replacing state files — src/state/store.ts:45 - [P1] Preserve SDK state-file modes too... (sha=be549f74007a2d8ef2e85239a6b57770bf6326d0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds same-directory temp-file/fsync/rename helpers for internal, CLI, and SDK state writes and adds concurrent read/write regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main writes state with direct fsp.writeFile and then reads it with JSON.parse, so a truncated state file propagates as a parse failure. I did not run a destructive SIGKILL or power-loss harness during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer action is needed for real proof, conflict resolution, and the permission/security fixes before this can be considered merge-ready.

target.title: fix(state): write state atomically to prevent corruption on termination
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes non-atomic Lobster state writes by staging JSON to a sibling temp file, fsyncing it, then renaming it over the target.
- Routes `writeStateJson`, `state.set`, SDK `stateSet`, and SDK `writeState` through atomic replacement.
- Preserves existing state-file modes across replacement and creates new state files as `0600`.
- Removes sibling temp files on failed write, close, chmod, or rename paths.
- Keeps approval-index exclusive-create semantics unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #108.
Closes #109.

## Maintainer repair

This branch was refreshed onto current `main` and the ClawSweeper findings were addressed:

- Existing file modes are preserved in both helper copies.
- New state files are private by default.
- Temp files are cleaned up on every failed path after staging begins.
- Added tests for atomic read/write behavior, mode preservation, private new-file modes, and failed-replacement cleanup in both store and SDK paths.
- Added an Unreleased changelog entry thanking @KrasimirKralev.

## Proof

Local verification on macOS, Node from repo toolchain:

```text
pnpm run typecheck
pass

node --test dist/test/state.test.js dist/test/resume.test.js dist/test/multi_approval_resume.test.js dist/test/approve_preview.test.js
pass: 14/14

pnpm run lint
pass: 0 errors, existing warnings only

pnpm run test
pass: 242/242
```

Real built SDK proof outside the test harness:

```text
LOBSTER_STATE_DIR=<tmp> node --input-type=module <script importing ./dist/src/sdk/primitives/state.js>
{"readBack":{"ok":true,"n":2},"firstMode":"600","secondMode":"600"}
```

That proof writes state through the built SDK, replaces it, reads it back, and confirms the file remains private before and after replacement.

Autoreview:

```text
/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.84)
```
target.author: krasimirkralev
